Container.Name: Hermes-Agent
Container.Overview: The self-improving AI agent built by Nous Research. The only agent with a built-in learning loop — persistent memory, 68+ skills, MCP support, and realtime voice plugins. [br]This template configures Hermes to run headlessly with the web dashboard exposed via basic auth, and is designed to pair with a local LLM (e.g. llama-swap on the same host). [br]After first install, run `hermes config set ...` in the container console to point the LLM endpoint at your local model server — or keep the default (OpenRouter + claude-opus). See the config-examples/ directory for a ready-made config.
Container.Description: Hermes Agent is Nous Research's self-improving AI agent. [br]Key features: [br]- Persistent 3-tier memory (episodic, semantic, procedural) [br]- 68+ built-in skills (coding, research, creative, MCP servers, etc.) [br]- Self-learning loop — discovers and stores new skills automatically [br]- MCP client + server for tool extensibility [br]- Real-time voice plugins (hermes-s2s, hermes-livekit) [br]- 22 messaging platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.) [br]- Home Assistant native tools [br][br]This template runs the agent in a headless-supervisor mode: the s6 init system keeps the dashboard and gateway running as supervised services, while the main container process idles. The web UI provides a full chat interface, config editor, and session browser. [br][br]Point it at a local OpenAI-compatible LLM (llama-swap, vLLM, etc.) for fully local operation, or use the built-in Nous Portal / OpenRouter integration for cloud models.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Single source of truth for all Hermes state: config, sessions, memories, skills, pairing codes. Must be owned by HERMES_UID:HERMES_GID (default 10000:10000).

Config.Name: Dashboard Port
Config.Description: Web dashboard HTTP port. Open http://[UNRAID-IP]:9119 in your browser after starting.

Config.Name: API Port
Config.Description: Hermes API server port (optional, set API_SERVER_ENABLED=true to enable)

Config.Name: HERMES_DASHBOARD
Config.Description: Set to 1 to launch the web dashboard alongside the main command

Config.Name: Dashboard Username
Config.Description: Basic auth username for the dashboard. Dashboard binds to 0.0.0.0 and requires auth — set both username and password.

Config.Name: Dashboard Password
Config.Description: Basic auth password for the dashboard. CHANGE THIS from the default. Dashboard binds to 0.0.0.0 and requires auth.

Config.Name: API_SERVER_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enable the API server (port 8642). When true, you can send requests to the Hermes API programmatically.

Config.Name: API_SERVER_KEY
Config.Description: Bearer token for API server auth. Required if API_SERVER_ENABLED=true.

Config.Name: HERMES_DASHBOARD_HOST
Config.Description: Bind address for the dashboard. 0.0.0.0 = all interfaces (LAN accessible with basic auth). 127.0.0.1 = localhost only (use SSH tunnel).

Config.Name: HERMES_DASHBOARD_PORT
Config.Description: Port for the dashboard HTTP server

Config.Name: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
Config.Description: Telegram bot token (from @BotFather). Leave blank if not using Telegram.

Config.Name: TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS
Config.Description: Comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to use the bot

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone for logs and cron scheduling

Config.Name: HERMES_UID
Config.Description: Runtime user ID. Must be 1000-65534. The appdata directory must be owned by this UID.

Config.Name: HERMES_GID
Config.Description: Runtime group ID. Must be 1000-65534. The appdata directory must be owned by this GID.
